name: Japanese
script: Japanese (漢字 kanji + ひらがな + カタカナ mixed)
register: Reverent contemporary Japanese matching the 新共同訳 (New Interconfessional Translation, 1987) — the Bible read across both Protestant and Catholic churches — carried into the warm second-person intimacy of the beloved 主の祈り (Lord's Prayer, 「天におられるわたしたちの父よ」). Divine persons take the honorific 御 (御父 / 御子); わたし is written in hiragana per that tradition.
quote_convention: Primary speech in 「 」 (kagi-kakko); a quotation nested inside a quotation in 『 』. Match the English source in count and placement.
poetry_convention: Preserve the English poetry line breaks exactly — one Japanese line per English line, same count, order, and copied numeric indent; only the line text is translated. Verse lines carry no terminal 。; internal 、 only where sense requires. Do not merge or split lines unless a native reviewer rebreaks them.

glossary.t: 天におられるわたしたちの父
glossary.gloss: our Father who is in heaven
glossary.note: Anchored to 新共同訳 (Mt 6:9) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "わたしたちの天の父". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)
Primary filial divine name. NEVER revert to 神 (God) or 主 (LORD).
Filial divine name (definite). Honorific 御 marks the divine Father over a generic 父; reviewer to confirm 御父 vs plain 父 (bare 新共同訳 usage).
Filial divine name; vocative form 天の父よ. Standard Bible phrase (Matt 6), never 父なる神 (would reintroduce 神).

Anchored to 新共同訳 (Rom 8:15 / Mk 14:36) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "アバ、父よ". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)
REVERSION TOKEN. Non-filial contrast term. Use ONLY where the English retains 'God' as God-as-God; never as a substitute for 'our Father'.

REVERSION TOKEN. Non-filial contrast term. Japanese renders the covenant name YHWH as 主. Use ONLY where the English keeps 'the LORD'; never as a substitute for 'our Father'. (The generic title 'Lord' for Jesus is also 主 but distinct in reference.)
Confirmed in preview (John 3). Standard across all Japanese Bibles.
Relational, not by-identity — 父 stem, not 聖霊. Confirmed in preview (Genesis 1:2).
Confirmed in preview (John 3). Standard Japanese title.
The Son (Jesus). Honorific 御子 is the settled 新共同訳 form for the divine Son.

glossary.gloss: the Son of God / child of God
glossary.note: Christological title retaining 'God'. Proposed — native reviewer to confirm 神の子 vs a filial 父の御子 (the Father's Son).
Confirmed in preview (John 3:16). The beloved 新共同訳 word 独り子.
FHB renders 'kingdom of God' filially: standard 神の国 → 父の国. Confirmed in preview (John 3:3,5).
Confirmed in preview. Standard katakana.
Confirmed in preview (John 3:28).
Proposed. 新共同訳 transliteration (John 1:41; 4:25), distinct from キリスト.
Proposed. Standard Japanese Bible term for both old and new covenant.
Renders Hebrew hesed. 慈しみ is the traditional 新共同訳 hesed word; reviewer to confirm vs 変わらぬ愛 (unchanging love).
Renders racham / splanchnizomai (gut-level tender mercy); the Gospel verb 憐れむ. Proposed.
Proposed. Standard Japanese Bible term.
Confirmed in preview (John 3:16). 新共同訳 phrasing.
Confirmed in preview (John 3:3,7). Preserve the John 3 double sense of 上から (from above) in a footnote.

doctrine_blocklist.use_instead: 新たに生まれる (born anew, Jn 3:3)
目が開かれる / 知る (per context)
